_comment: sluice browser seccomp profile (SLUICE_SECCOMP=browser). This is core/seccomp.json MINUS the calls a browser engine (Chromium/Playwright) needs to build its OWN unprivileged-userns sandbox: unshare, clone(CLONE_NEWUSER), clone3, mount, umount2, pivot_root. Everything else the hardened profile blocks stays blocked (setns, bpf, ptrace, perf_event_open, keyctl family, the new mount API, modules, kexec/swap, userfaultfd, kcmp, *_handle_at, personality ADDR_NO_RANDOMIZE, ...). Use this only when an agent must run a browser WITH its internal sandbox; otherwise run the browser --no-sandbox under hardened (the container is already the jail). Behavioural check against a real Chromium/Playwright run is manual - CI has no browser fixture. Targets amd64/arm64.

syscalls._comment: namespace setns, tracing, key material, the NEW mount API, modules, kexec/swap. (classic mount/umount2/pivot_root + unshare/clone(NEWUSER) are intentionally allowed here for the browser sandbox.)
the rest of the OCI default deny set: non-cap-gated exploitation primitives + obsolete/cap-gated calls (kept identical to hardened).
block personality() only when it sets ADDR_NO_RANDOMIZE (0x0040000 = 262144); kept identical to hardened.
